mnemonics: ROY G. BIV for Time Series: Remember the order of operations: Read data, Observe patterns (plot), Your model (decompose/ARIMA), Get forecasts, Beware of assumptions, Inspect residuals, Validate model.
keyPoints: The ts() function is used to create a time series object in R, requiring the data, start time, and frequency.
Decomposition (decompose()) separates a series into Trend, Seasonal, and Random components to understand its structure.
The Box-Jenkins methodology for ARIMA modeling involves Identification (ACF/PACF), Estimation, and Diagnostic Checking.
commonMistakes: Using an ARIMA model without checking for stationarity first (e.g., forgetting to difference the data).
Ignoring residual analysis; a good forecast model should have residuals that resemble white noise.
Misinterpreting the ACF/PACF plots, leading to the wrong choice of p and q parameters for the ARIMA model.
